description: Auth Activation Review — credential_file.py bootstrap gap analysis

security_analysis.secret_handling: password written to file only, never to stdout/stderr
security_analysis.logging_behavior: NONE — caller logs the path (not the password)

credential_file_review.function_signature: write_initial_credentials(email: str, password: str, *, label: str = 'initial') -> Path
credential_file_review.side_effects: file write only, no DB, no network, no init_engine
credential_file_review.secret_leak_risk: none (0600 + operator picks up from file)

private_main_bootstrap_gap.private_local_auth_router: EXISTS (untracked, working dir) — has /register but no /initialize-admin

bootstrap_chain_analysis.origin_main_flow: credential_file.py writes creds → operator reads file → calls POST /initialize-admin → create_user(admin)
bootstrap_chain_analysis.local_working_dir_flow: /register creates user (not admin) — no admin creation path

reset_admin_deferral.reason: production_db_surface — calls init_engine_from_config(config.database), direct DB user manipulation. CLI operational tool, NOT part of auth activation gate. Defer to future operational tooling phase.

strategy_a.name: Port credential_file.py as isolated low-risk file
strategy_a.reason: credential_file.py alone cannot complete bootstrap — needs /initialize-admin endpoint and count_admin_users() method

strategy_b.name: Port credential_file.py + initialize_admin endpoint + count_admin_users
strategy_b.scope: Add credential_file.py, add /initialize-admin router, add count_admin_users to LocalAuthProvider
strategy_b.reason: Full bootstrap chain. Requires engine initialization to work (production_DB constraint). Safe for controlled activation path.

strategy_c.name: Defer credential bootstrap until production SQLite review
strategy_c.reason: Preserves blockers but leaves BLOCKING gap open

integration_strategy.reason: Strategy A only solves half the problem. Strategy B completes the bootstrap chain properly. credential_file.py is confirmed LOW risk (no DB, no init_engine). The /initialize-admin endpoint and count_admin_users() require engine initialization which is already blocked by production SQLite/Postgres blockers.
integration_strategy.conditional_note: Strategy B execution requires explicit authorization per-file. R241-25J authorization package scopes per-file.

next_prompt: Enter R241-25J_CREDENTIAL_BOOTSTRAP_PORT. R241-25I complete. credential_file.py confirmed LOW risk (no DB, no init_engine). Full bootstrap requires 3 components: credential_file.py + initialize-admin endpoint + count_admin_users(). Strategy B recommended (full port). R241-25J authorization package ready. Requires explicit user authorization per-file before execution. All blockers preserved.
